title: AndroidCommonDoc Agents Manifest
description: Single source of truth for all L0 agent metadata. CI validators MUST validate the manifest against this schema; a green schema check is the precondition for any agent template / mirror / registry write.

paths_template.description: Path templates derived from canonical_name. Per-agent paths_override supported.

categories.description: Enum of legal `category` values for agent entries. Adding a new category requires schema_version bump.

invariants.description: Cross-cutting rules enforced by the manifest validator. Each invariant has an id, an applies_to selector, and a require constraint.

applies_to.description: Selector — agent matches when ALL key/value pairs equal the agent's value.

require.description: Constraints that must hold for matched agents. Keys are dotted paths into the agent entry; values are expected sets/values.

rationale_ref.description: Optional pointer to memory/backlog/doc explaining the rule's origin.

canonical_name.description: Used in SendMessage(to=...). MUST equal the YAML key. MUST equal subagent_type in v1.

subagent_type.description: Used in Agent(subagent_type=...). MUST equal canonical_name in v1.

template_frontmatter_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the template's frontmatter block (between --- markers). Used for drift detection. NULL allowed during seed phase; required in Phase 4.

category.description: Must be a member of top-level `categories` array.

lifecycle.description: ephemeral = spawned per dispatch and dies; persistent = lives across phases (e.g., context-provider); per-task = spawned per Task tool invocation.

description.description: One-line, copied verbatim from the template's frontmatter `description:` field.

allowed.description: Exact, expanded list from frontmatter `tools:` field.

top_level.description: Top-level tool names (e.g., Agent, Write, Edit). Hook blocks PreToolUse on these.

bash_subcommands.description: Bash subcommand names (e.g., grep, rg, find). Hook blocks Bash invocations whose first non-flag arg matches.

grep_patterns.description: Glob patterns blocking Grep tool by path. `*` denotes any path.

glob_patterns.description: Glob patterns blocking Glob tool by pattern. e.g., `docs/**`.

read_patterns.description: Glob patterns blocking Read tool by path. e.g., `docs/**` blocks reading any pattern doc directly. Surfaced in 4 core-specialist templates (W26→W31).

mcp_tools_blocked.description: Specific MCP tool names this agent must NOT call. e.g., `mcp__androidcommondoc__find-pattern` for specialists. Stricter than excluding from `tools.allowed` (catches frontmatter-inheritance edge cases).

spawn_method.description: How this agent enters the session. Most are spawned via Agent(); team-lead is manual-cli (the main agent itself).

dispatched_by.description: Canonical names of agents allowed to spawn / dispatch this agent.

can_dispatch_to.description: Canonical names this agent can spawn/dispatch. Empty for in-process peers (no Agent tool).

can_send_to.description: SendMessage targets. `*` means broadcast to all session peers (used by context-provider).

triggers.description: Tool names or `Tool:subcommand` patterns that activate this gate.

resolution.description: Action the agent must take to pass the gate.

hook_path.description: Path to the hook script that enforces this gate.

memory.description: Frontmatter `memory:` value (e.g., 'project'). Null if not declared.

aliases.description: MUST be empty in v1. Reserved for future renames.

superseded_by.description: If non-null, this agent has been replaced by the named agent and should not be spawned in new sessions.

replaces.description: If non-null, this agent replaces the named legacy agent. Validator scans memory/backlog for legacy mentions and flags them.
